_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Determine if security engineering is used when acquiring or developing protection and detection capabilities
reference_document_element_description: Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI technologies, including foundation models, autonomous agents, and AI-generated content, to both technical and executive audiences.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Ability SP-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-003 (SP, cosine: 0.463), SP-T-001 (SP, cosine: 0.457).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.6/10 (raw 0.257). Shared vocabulary: [security]. NICE-only terms [acquiring, capabilities, detection, determine, developing, engineering] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, agents, ai, assess, audiences, autonomous] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.850, R=0.846). Balanced P/R — the 14-word NICE element and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Determine if security engineering is used when acquiring or developing protectio" and "Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI techn" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.479 > STS 0.257 (gap +0.222). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [security] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.7%. Shared tokens: [security]. NICE-only: [acquiring, capabilities, detection, determine, developing, engineering]. WIDAI-only: [ability, agents, ai, assess, audiences, autonomous].
